page: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The configuration features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and is equipped with a 154 GB local storage disk. The instance is Premium Storage capable but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1409.59, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1277.0, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3678.27 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2826.0. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, measuring 27.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 21.12 GB/sec for large-block writes. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to a poor rating.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4ds_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated processing power without extreme multi-threaded demands. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations. However, it is less optimal for memory-intensive workloads, high-throughput database systems, or heavy multi-threaded compilation tasks due to its lower memory bandwidth and weaker multi-core benchmark results.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv4 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 154 GB local storage disk, and is Premium Storage capable, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1277.0 and average single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weaker, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 2826.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 27.12 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced, general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and text processing. It is less suited for highly parallelized applications or memory-intensive database operations due to its moderate multi-core and memory throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, delivering average single-core performance for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, running on an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Includes 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
Delivers average single-core performance but weaker multi-core throughput
Best suited for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and text processing
